Interior Plaster in Comal County, TX

Star Stucco of Austin provides interior plaster services to homeowners throughout Comal County. From the German-heritage architecture of New Braunfels to the waterfront properties around Canyon Lake, we deliver wall finishes that bring depth, texture, and enduring quality to your interiors.

Get a Free EstimateCall (512) 706-9699

Old World Plaster Craft for Comal County Interiors

Comal County carries a strong architectural legacy rooted in its German and Czech settlement history. The stone homes, timber-framed buildings, and plaster-finished interiors that defined early New Braunfels construction are still part of the county's visual identity. Interior plaster connects modern homeowners to that tradition while delivering the durability and beauty that today's materials make possible.

New Braunfels has grown substantially, and neighborhoods like Vintage Oaks, River Chase, and Gruene Forest feature custom and semi-custom homes where interior plaster elevates the standard builder finish. Meanwhile, the Canyon Lake corridor attracts homeowners building retreat-style properties with open great rooms and large windows — spaces where plaster walls catch natural light and create a warm, inviting atmosphere.

Our team has worked in Comal County across a wide range of project types, from restoring original plaster walls in downtown New Braunfels buildings to finishing entire new homes in the developments expanding along the I-35 corridor. We bring the tools, products, and hands-on expertise to handle any interior plaster project in the area.

What's Included With Our Interior Plaster in Comal County

Every project includes expert craftsmanship, quality materials, and a commitment to lasting results.

Design Consultation

We schedule an on-site meeting at your Comal County home to review wall surfaces, discuss your preferences, and present finish samples that show texture, color, and sheen.

Surface Preparation

All walls are repaired, cleaned, and treated with bonding primer. We take special care to protect natural stone, hardwood floors, and custom millwork during preparation.

Base Coat Application

A high-quality base coat is applied to establish a uniform bonding surface. We select products that pair correctly with the chosen finish coat and the existing wall substrate.

Decorative Finish Application

The finish coat is hand-troweled in one to several passes depending on the desired look. Burnishing, layering, and tinting techniques are used as needed to build the final surface.

Color Matching & Tinting

Pigments are folded into the plaster so color is integral, not surface-applied. We work with you to develop hues that complement the natural stone and wood common in Comal County homes.

Protective Sealing & Cleanup

The completed surface is sealed to protect against everyday wear and moisture. All protective coverings are removed, and we leave your home clean and move-in ready.

Common Interior Plaster Needs in Comal County

Comal County homeowners request interior plaster for everything from single accent features to whole-home finishes. Here are the projects we see most frequently.

Smooth Wall Finishes

Many newer homes in Comal County subdivisions come with standard spray textures. A smooth skim coat replaces that rough surface with a clean, contemporary finish that makes rooms feel open and polished.

Decorative Plaster Accents

Entryway walls, fireplace surrounds, and dining room features are popular accent projects in New Braunfels custom homes. Decorative plaster adds handcrafted character to these high-visibility areas.

Venetian Plaster

The polished, layered quality of Venetian plaster suits the European-influenced architecture found throughout Comal County. It is especially popular in formal rooms and primary suite retreats.

Interior Wall Restoration

Some of New Braunfels' oldest buildings retain original plaster walls that have developed cracks and soft spots over time. We restore these surfaces using techniques compatible with the original materials.

Custom Textures for Feature Walls

Whether the goal is a rough Old World texture in a wine cellar or a smooth metallic accent in a modern media room, we create custom surfaces tailored to the room and your taste.

Skim Coating Over Textured Walls

A smooth skim coat over dated texture is a straightforward way to modernize interiors throughout Comal County without replacing the underlying drywall.

Why Choose Us for Interior Plaster in Comal County?

  • Artisan-level craft that honors Comal County's rich architectural heritage while using modern materials
  • Full spectrum of finish styles from rustic lime plaster to refined Venetian polish
  • Sophisticated color matching for seamless integration with stone, wood, and existing decor
  • Clean, methodical work practices that keep your home orderly throughout the project
  • Precise attention to every joint, edge, and corner for a finished product that looks intentional
  • Custom approaches designed around your home's specific architecture, lighting, and interior design

Licensed & Insured

Full liability coverage and workers' compensation for every project in Comal County.

20+ Years Experience

Decades of hands-on stucco work across Central Texas residential and commercial properties.

Certified Applicators

Certified by Sto Corp, Senergy/BASF, Master Wall, Dryvit, and Parex LaHabra for the highest quality results.

Our Interior Plaster Process

A straightforward approach from start to finish — no surprises, no shortcuts.

1

Design Consultation

We meet at your Comal County home, review the walls, and walk through physical samples so you can compare textures and colors in the actual space where they will be applied.

2

Surface Preparation & Priming

All surfaces are repaired and primed. Stone, hardwood, and other finishes common in New Braunfels homes are carefully masked before any material is applied.

3

Plaster Application & Finishing

Plaster is applied in measured coats, each one contributing to the final texture and color depth. Venetian and polished finishes receive additional burnishing passes.

4

Final Review & Touch-Ups

We review every wall with you, checking consistency under different light sources. Any spots that need refinement are addressed before sealing.
